title: Basic operators
_authoring: Pedagogical full pass (curriculum audit). Added intro with lessonIntro, per-KC explanations (AND/OR/NOT), and a worked example (A AND B traced four rows). Items reordered: AND → OR → NOT → review. Added l1-review-mix (kept tier 1 so L1 stays single-tier and the hardest-tier streak gate remains reachable) at the end to revisit AND/OR/NOT in one mixed expression after NOT is established (within-lesson spacing). OR explanation explicitly confronts inclusive-vs-exclusive confusion. All truthTables verified MSB-first.

lessonIntro.title: Lesson 1 — Basic Operators: AND, OR, NOT
lessonIntro.body: Boolean logic is the algebra of true and false. Every circuit, every conditional in code, every search filter ultimately reduces to three primitive operations: AND, OR, and NOT. In this lesson you will build and read truth tables for all three — the skill that underlies every lesson that follows.

explanations.body: AND produces true ONLY when BOTH inputs are true. If either input is false, the output is false. Think of it as a chain of requirements: A AND B is satisfied only when A is met AND B is met. One broken link, the whole thing fails.

OR produces true when AT LEAST ONE input is true — including when both are true. This is the inclusive OR of logic, and it differs from everyday English 'or', which usually means 'one or the other but not both'. In logic, OR(true, true) = true. The only case where OR is false is when every input is false.

NOT flips the value: NOT true = false, NOT false = true. It is a unary operator (takes exactly one input). In circuit diagrams it is drawn as a bubble or inverter. In pseudocode it is written NOT A or !A.

steps.label: List all input combinations
steps.detail: With two variables A and B, there are 2² = 4 rows. List them MSB-first: (A=0,B=0), (A=0,B=1), (A=1,B=0), (A=1,B=1).

steps.label: Apply AND to each row
steps.detail: Row 1: 0 AND 0 = 0. Row 2: 0 AND 1 = 0. Row 3: 1 AND 0 = 0. Row 4: 1 AND 1 = 1. AND is true only in the last row — when both inputs are 1.

steps.label: Read the output column
steps.detail: The truth table for A AND B is [0, 0, 0, 1]. Only one 1 out of four rows — AND is demanding: both inputs must be true.

steps.label: Recognise the circuit and pseudocode forms
steps.detail: In a circuit this is a 2-input AND gate with A and B as inputs and the output wire carrying the result. In pseudocode: 'if A AND B then ...' — the body runs only when both flags are set.
